sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.88 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: (G.\,M.\,Bergman). The width of a group$G$with respect to a generating set$X$means the supremum over all$g G$of the least length of a group word in elements of$X$expressing$g$. A group$G$has finite width if the width of$G$with respect to every generating set is finite. Does there exist a countably infinite group of finite width? All known infinite groups of finite width (infinite permutation group...
